steps.title: Identify the equation of the line
steps.content:
From the graph, the line has slope $\tfrac{1}{2}$ and y-intercept $1$. Its equation is:
$$y = \tfrac{1}{2}x + 1$$

steps.title: Substitute the coordinates (7, c) into the equation
steps.content:
The point $(7, c)$ lies on the line, so it must satisfy the equation. Substitute $x = 7$:
$$c = \tfrac{1}{2}(7) + 1 = 3.5 + 1 = 4.5$$
So $c = \tfrac{9}{2}$ or equivalently $4.5$. The answer is 4.5.
